free_text: Study of the 14C(p,n)14N and 14C(a,n)17O Reactions
Targets of 14C were prepared
by running a high-frequency discharge in acetylene
at a pressure of about 0.7 mm  Hg between target
electrodes containing the 10-mil tungsten
backings spaced 1 cm apart. The acetylene was
converted from barium carbonate enriched to 38.7% in
14C . The resulting residues on tungsten backing was
assumed to be carbon of the same isotopic constitution
as the barium carbonate.No correction for possible
target contamination was applied to the results.
No information given
Compiled by S.H.

Data taken from tab.4 of the reference PR 104.
The neutron detector was a National Radiac
Company Type NSS-1 boron-loaded ZnS(Ag) scintillator
on a 6467 photomultiplier tube embeded in a paraffin
moderator. The dependence of its efficiency
upon neutron energy was not known.
